title: DAY 1: Foundations – The Freelancer Mindset & Finding Your Niche
objective: By the end of Day 1, you will understand the freelancing landscape, define your niche, and clarify your service menu.

detailedContent.title: Freelancing Is a Business, Not a Hobby
detailedContent.content:
You are now a business owner. You don't just 'do' the work; you sell, manage finances, and market yourself.

**The Niche Advantage:**
Clients hire specialists, not generalists. A niche makes you memorable, justifies higher rates, and simplifies marketing.

**Finding Your Niche:**
Identify the intersection of:
1. What you're good at.
2. What you enjoy doing.
3. What people will pay for.

detailedContent.title: Defining Your Service Menu
detailedContent.content: Don't say 'I do graphic design.' Say 'I design brand identities for eco-friendly startups.' Create a clear list of 3-5 specific offerings to help clients understand your value immediately.

video.title: How to Find Your Freelance Niche
video.description: Flux Academy explains the strategic importance of narrowing your focus to earn more.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Skills & Passion Audit (10 minutes)
steps: List every technical and soft skill you possess
Highlight the ones you genuinely enjoy performing

exercises.title: Exercise 2: The Niche Statement (10 minutes)
steps: Write your niche statement: 'I help [Target Audience] achieve [Outcome] by [Service]'

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Service Menu Creation (10 minutes)
steps: Draft 3-5 specific services with clear deliverables (e.g., '1,500-word SEO blog post')

reflectionPrompt: How does it feel to transition from a generalist to a specialist? What's one industry I'm genuinely curious about?

title: DAY 2: Building Your Toolkit – Profiles & Portfolio
objective: By the end of Day 2, you will set up professional profiles and start a portfolio that attracts clients.

detailedContent.title: Your Online Storefront
detailedContent.content:
**LinkedIn (Non-negotiable):**
- **Headline:** Focus on value, not just title. 'B2B Writer helping SaaS companies' vs 'Freelance Writer'.
- **About Section:** Tell a story, include a CTA.

**The Minimum Viable Portfolio:**
- Use free tools like Behance (design), Contently (writing), or a simple Google Drive folder.
- **No client work?** Create 'Spec Work'—samples designed for your dream clients to show what you *could* do.

video.title: How to Create a Freelance Portfolio (No Experience)
video.description: Strategies for building a body of work when you're just starting out.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: LinkedIn Optimization (15 minutes)
steps: Update your LinkedIn headline using the Day 1 niche statement
Add a professional headshot and a value-driven 'About' section

exercises.title: Exercise 2: The Spec Work Sample (20 minutes)
steps: Create one high-quality sample piece for a fictional client in your niche

reflectionPrompt: Would I hire me based on my current LinkedIn profile? What's the biggest gap I see?

title: DAY 3: Finding Clients – Outreach & Pitching
objective: By the end of Day 3, you will have a prospects list and know how to craft pitches that get responses.

detailedContent.title: Where Clients Come From
detailedContent.content:
1. **Your Network:** Warm leads are the easiest to close.
2. **Inbound:** Content that makes clients find you.
3. **Job Boards/Platforms:** Upwork, Fiverr, ProBlogger.
4. **Cold Outreach:** Direct, personalized pitching.

**The Perfect Pitch:**
- Be personalized (no copy-pasting).
- Focus on THEIR problem, not your experience.
- Offer a clear, low-pressure CTA (e.g., a 10-minute chat).

video.title: Cold Email That Actually Gets Clients
video.description: Alex Cattoni breaks down the anatomy of a high-response pitch.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Dream 20 List (15 minutes)
steps: Identify 20 companies or individuals who fit your Ideal Client Profile

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Pitch Customization (20 minutes)
steps: Draft 3 personalized pitches for individuals on your Dream 20 list

reflectionPrompt: What is my biggest fear regarding cold outreach? How can I reframe 'rejection' as 'data'?

title: DAY 4: Pricing & Proposals
objective: By the end of Day 4, you will set your rates and create winning proposals.

detailedContent.title: Pricing Models
detailedContent.content:
- **Hourly:** Safe for beginners, bad for efficiency.
- **Project-Based:** Aligns with value, better for fast workers.
- **Value-Based:** Multiplier based on the client's ROI (Advanced).

**The Winning Proposal Structure:**
1. **The Problem:** Show you listened.
2. **The Solution:** Clear deliverables and process.
3. **The Investment:** Pricing with options (Good/Better/Best).
4. **Proof:** Testimonials and case studies.

video.title: How to Price Your Freelance Services
video.description: The Futur's guide to moving from hourly rates to value-based pricing.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Rate Calculation (10 minutes)
steps: Determine your desired income + 30% for taxes/fees, then divide by billable hours

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Create a Proposal Template (20 minutes)
steps: Design a repeatable structure in Google Docs or Canva including summary, timeline, and investment

reflectionPrompt: Am I underpricing because of a lack of skill or a lack of confidence?

title: DAY 5: Contracts, Invoices, & Getting Paid
objective: By the end of Day 5, you will have the templates to protect yourself and manage your finances.

detailedContent.title: No Contract, No Work
detailedContent.content:
A contract protects both parties by setting expectations for scope, revisions, and payment terms.

**Invoicing Basics:**
- Get a deposit upfront (25-50%).
- Use tools like Wave, FreshBooks, or simple Stripe/PayPal invoices.
- Set aside 30% of every payment for future taxes immediately.

video.title: Freelance Contracts & Invoicing 101
video.description: Essential legal and financial guardrails for every independent professional.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Contract Customization (15 minutes)
steps: Find a free freelancer contract and customize it with your specific terms and revision policy

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Setting Your Tax System (10 minutes)
steps: Decide exactly where you will store your 30% tax set-aside for each payment

reflectionPrompt: Why is asking for a deposit upfront a sign of a professional freelancer?

title: DAY 6: Project Management & Client Relations
objective: By the end of Day 6, you will have systems to manage work and build long-term relationships.

detailedContent.title: Clients Hire Results
detailedContent.content:
Professionalism is as important as project quality.

**Relationship Keys:**
- **Underpromise/Overdeliver:** Realistic deadlines + small surprises.
- **Boundaries:** Set communication hours. No midnight Slack messages.
- **Status Updates:** Weekly emails prevent the 'where is my project' anxiety.

video.title: How to Manage Multiple Freelance Clients
video.description: Systems and tools to keep projects moving without burning out.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Onboarding Checklist (10 minutes)
steps: Create a step-by-step list of what happens from a signed contract to project kick-off

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Communication Boundaries (5 minutes)
steps: Write down your office hours and expected response times for clients

reflectionPrompt: How can I make my clients feel supported without sacrificing my own personal time?

title: DAY 7: Final Project – Your 30-Day Launch Plan
objective: By the end of Day 7, you will have a complete actionable roadmap for your first month of freelancing.

detailedContent.title: Launch Readiness
detailedContent.content:
**Your 30-Day Plan includes:**
1. Finalized Niche & Services.
2. Optimized Professional Profiles.
3. Your Dream 20 Prospect list.
4. Daily Outreach goals (e.g., 2 pitches/day).
5. Weekly content schedule to build authority.

video.title: How to Launch Your Freelance Business in 30 Days
video.description: A comprehensive roadmap for going from zero to your first paid gig.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The 30-Day Action Plan (90+ minutes)
steps: Synthesize your work from the week into a week-by-week calendar of tasks
Set a specific, measurable goal for Month 1 (e.g., land 1 project or 5 discovery calls)

reflectionPrompt: Look back at Day 1. What is the biggest mindset shift I've experienced this week?
